# runtime-go

Go runtime for code generated by the [Vexil](https://github.com/vexil-lang/vexil) schema compiler. This package is a dependency of generated Go code -- you add it alongside code produced by `vexilc codegen --target go`.

Zero dependencies. Go 1.22+.

## Install

```sh
go get github.com/vexil-lang/vexil/packages/runtime-go
```

## API

### BitWriter / BitReader

LSB-first bit-level I/O. Sub-byte fields accumulate within a byte; multi-byte writes are little-endian.

```go
import vexil "github.com/vexil-lang/vexil/packages/runtime-go"

w := vexil.NewBitWriter()
w.WriteBits(5, 4)       // 4 bits
w.WriteU16(1000)        // 2 bytes LE
w.WriteVarint(42)       // unsigned LEB128
w.WriteZigzag(-7)       // ZigZag-encoded signed LEB128
w.WriteString("hello")  // length-prefixed UTF-8
bytes := w.Finish()

r := vexil.NewBitReader(bytes)
bits := r.ReadBits(4)
val := r.ReadU16()
v := r.ReadVarint()
s := r.ReadZigzag()
str := r.ReadString()
```

### Packer / Unpacker interfaces

Generated structs implement `Packer` and `Unpacker`:

```go
type Packer interface {
    Pack(w *BitWriter) error
}

type Unpacker interface {
    Unpack(r *BitReader) error
}
```

### SchemaHandshake

Exchanges BLAKE3 schema hashes between peers to detect mismatches before data transfer:

```go
h := vexil.NewSchemaHandshake(schemaHash)
offer := h.CreateOffer()     // send to peer
ok := h.Accept(offer)        // peer validates hash match
```

## Wire compatibility

`BitWriter` and `BitReader` produce and consume the same byte sequences as the Rust [`vexil-runtime`](https://crates.io/crates/vexil-runtime) crate and the TypeScript [`@vexil-lang/runtime`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@vexil-lang/runtime) package. This is verified by the [compliance vector suite](https://github.com/vexil-lang/vexil/tree/main/compliance/vectors).

## Safety limits

- Max recursion depth: 64
- Max byte array / string length: 16 MiB
- Max collection count: 1,048,576 elements
